Name: Free Worlds League
Description: The Free Worlds League is one of the five Successor States of the Star League, located rimward and anti-spinward of Terra. Ruled by House Marik, the League was, with the Capellan Confederation, one of the original signatories of the treaties that would eventually form the Star League. After the fall of the Star League, the Free Worlds League granted broad control over its economy and government to the commander of its military, the Captain-General. The League is known for being a powerful mercantile state, but the Succession Wars have nearly destroyed its economy. The current ruler of the League is Captain-General Janos Marik.
ReputationStatements: I don't know why we bother offering you contracts, to be perfectly honest. It's obvious you're not a friend to the Free Worlds League. Regardless, we find ourselves in desperate times, so desperate measures.
This contract has already survived three committees and two political objections. Don’t make me regret that effort.
The League has learned to be cautious when hiring mercenaries with flexible loyalties. Our terms reflect this caution.
The Free Worlds League wishes to hire your services, Commander. As the leader of a respected mercenary company, you should find the terms both fair and equitable, I think.
Ordinarily, provincial forces would handle this operation. Unfortunately, the provinces rarely agree on anything. Try not to inflame local politics while you’re saving the region.
Your company has shown an understanding of both military and political realities. Still, I was astonished how rapidly the committee cleared this latest offer for you.
Greetings, my friend. This contract is not one we would usually offer a mercenary, but you've proven your loyalty, prowess, and dedication to the ideals of the Free Worlds League.
MissionSuccessStatements: Excellent work, Commander. Commercial traffic and regional stability should now both improve considerably now the matter is settled.
GoodFaithFailureStatements: Disappointing, but trade routes remain partially functional thanks to your actions. We understand that sacrifice on the battlefield doesn't always translate to victory.
BadFaithFailureStatements: We are deeply disappointed in your performance, Commander. Your retreat disrupted military coordination and regional commerce simultaneously. Impressive, in the worst way.
